message:
fix(algorithms): preserve runtime_policy through multigraph→simple conversions

The four multigraph-to-simple conversion helpers in fnx-python build a
new Graph/DiGraph from a source MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph, but only seeded
the policy via `with_runtime_policy(clone)` at construction. Any later
mutation (add_node_with_attrs, add_edge_with_attrs) could observe or
reset the effective runtime policy on the target, leaving the returned
simple graph without the source's policy attached.

Fix: capture the runtime_policy once, clone it into the constructor, and
then explicitly `set_runtime_policy(runtime_policy)` after the graph is
fully populated. Applied to all four helpers:

  - multigraph_to_simple_graph
  - multigraph_to_weighted_simple_graph
  - multidigraph_to_simple_digraph
  - multidigraph_to_weighted_simple_digraph

This guarantees that algorithms which internally collapse parallel edges
(e.g. weighted centrality on a MultiGraph) return a simple graph that
honors the caller's runtime policy — matching what a caller would see
if they had passed a simple graph in directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
